name: Aberdare National Park
description: A high-altitude park covering the Aberdare mountain range, home to rare mountain bongos, elephants, black rhinos, and spectacular waterfalls including Karuru and Gura Falls.

name: Amboseli National Park
description: One of Kenya's most iconic parks, famous for large elephant herds roaming freely against the backdrop of Mount Kilimanjaro. Excellent for lion sightings and photography.

name: Central Island National Park
description: A volcanic island in the middle of Lake Turkana with active craters, hot springs, and one of the world's largest Nile crocodile breeding grounds.

name: Chyulu Hills National Park
description: A volcanic range of green hills and lava caves bordering Tsavo West, home to elephants, lions, and zebras. Features the Leviathan Cave, one of the world's longest lava tubes.

name: Hell's Gate National Park
description: One of the few Kenyan parks where visitors can walk and cycle freely among wildlife. Famous for dramatic gorges, geothermal activity, and diverse birdlife including lammergeyers.

name: Kakamega Forest National Reserve
description: Kenya's only tropical rainforest and a remnant of the ancient Guineo-Congolian forest. Home to over 330 bird species, rare primates, and unique plant species found nowhere else in Kenya.

name: Kisite Mpunguti Marine Park & Reserve
description: A pristine marine park near Wasini Island, renowned for snorkeling, dolphin watching, and diverse coral reef ecosystems. One of Kenya's most biodiverse marine protected areas.

name: Kisumu Impala Sanctuary
description: A small but significant sanctuary on the shores of Lake Victoria in Kisumu, home to impalas, hippos, vervet monkeys, and over 100 bird species.

name: Kiunga Marine National Park & Reserve
description: A remote marine protected area along the northern Kenyan coast near the Somali border, known for dugongs, green turtles, whale sharks, and pristine coral reefs.

name: Kora National Park
description: A remote wilderness park on the banks of the Tana River, famous as the home of conservationist George Adamson who rehabilitated lions here. Home to lions, leopards, elephants, and over 200 bird species.

name: Lake Elementaita Wildlife Sanctuary
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and Ramsar wetland in the Rift Valley, famous for thousands of lesser flamingos, pelicans, and over 400 bird species on its alkaline shores.

name: Lake Nakuru National Park
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for its pink carpet of flamingos on the alkaline lake, and one of Kenya's best parks for white and black rhino sightings.

name: Malindi Marine National Park & Reserve
description: One of Africa's oldest marine parks, established in 1968, protecting coral reefs, sea turtles, whale sharks, and diverse reef fish along the Kilifi coastline.

name: Malka Mari National Park
description: A remote and rarely visited park in northern Kenya along the Ethiopian border, home to Beisa oryx, Grevy's zebras, lions, and diverse arid-land wildlife.

name: Marsabit National Park
description: A unique forested volcanic mountain rising from the arid northern plains, with crater lakes, dense forests, and famous large-tusked elephants. Home to lions, leopards, and diverse birdlife.

name: Meru National Park
description: A pristine wilderness park made famous by Joy and George Adamson's Elsa the lioness. Home to all Big Five, Grevy's zebras, reticulated giraffes, and diverse wildlife in an untouched savanna setting.

name: Mombasa Marine National Park & Reserve
description: A protected marine area along the Mombasa coast offering excellent snorkeling, glass-bottom boat rides, and diverse reef ecosystems with sea turtles and reef fish.

name: Mount Elgon National Park
description: A transboundary park straddling Kenya and Uganda on the slopes of Mount Elgon, an extinct volcano. Famous for elephants entering caves to mine salt, forest hiking, and diverse montane vegetation.

name: Mount Longonot National Park
description: A dormant stratovolcano in the Great Rift Valley offering spectacular crater rim hiking with panoramic views across the Rift Valley. Wildlife including buffaloes, zebras, and leopards found in the forest-filled crater.

name: Mount Kenya National Park
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site centred on Mount Kenya, Africa's second-highest peak at 5,199m. Offers mountaineering, trekking, and diverse afromontane ecosystems. Home to elephants, buffaloes, and rare mountain bongos.

name: Mwea National Reserve
description: A wetland reserve on the Tana River floodplains, important for hippos, crocodiles, and diverse waterbirds. Surrounded by Mwea irrigation rice paddies.

name: Nairobi Animal Orphanage
description: A wildlife rescue and rehabilitation center within Nairobi National Park caring for injured and orphaned animals including lions, cheetahs, and various wildlife before release back into the wild.

name: Nairobi National Park
description: The world's only national park within a capital city, home to lions, leopards, cheetahs, rhinos, buffaloes, giraffes, and over 400 bird species — all with a city skyline backdrop. Established in 1946.

name: Nairobi Safari Walk
description: An elevated boardwalk sanctuary adjacent to Nairobi National Park offering close-up encounters with Kenya's diverse ecosystems — savannah, swamp, and forest — in a controlled educational environment.

name: Nasolot National Reserve
description: A rugged and remote reserve in West Pokot with dramatic escarpments, riverine forests, and diverse wildlife including elephants, buffaloes, and rare bird species.

name: Ndere Island National Park
description: A small island park in Lake Victoria accessible by boat from Kisumu, home to hippos, impalas, monitor lizards, pythons, and over 100 bird species.

name: Ol Donyo Sabuk National Park
description: A scenic mountain park near Thika with forested slopes, waterfalls, and diverse wildlife including buffaloes, leopards, and over 300 bird species. Contains the grave of Lord William Northrup McMillan.

name: Ruma National Park
description: Kenya's only park where the rare roan antelope survives, set in the Lambwe Valley near Lake Victoria. Also home to oribi, topi, Rothschild giraffes, and over 400 bird species.

name: Saiwa Swamp National Park
description: Kenya's smallest national park and the only one gazetted specifically to protect the semi-aquatic sitatunga antelope. Set in a papyrus swamp with elevated boardwalks for wildlife viewing.

name: Shimba Hills National Reserve
description: A coastal forest reserve near Mombasa home to the rare sable antelope, forest elephants, leopards, and diverse coastal forest bird species. Features the spectacular Sheldrick Falls.

name: Sibiloi National Park
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site on the eastern shores of Lake Turkana, containing some of the world's most significant early human fossil sites. Home to Nile crocodiles, hippos, zebras, and diverse arid-land wildlife.

name: South Island National Park
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and volcanic island in Lake Turkana serving as a critical breeding ground for Nile crocodiles, hippos, and numerous waterbirds.

name: Tsavo East National Park
description: One of the world's largest national parks, famous for its red-dust-stained elephants, vast open savannahs, the Galana River, and Lugard Falls. Home to all Big Five and the legendary man-eating Tsavo lions.

name: Tsavo West National Park
description: A diverse park featuring the crystal-clear Mzima Springs where hippos and crocodiles can be viewed underwater, volcanic landscapes, rhino sanctuary, and dense wildlife populations including all Big Five.

name: Watamu Marine National Park & Reserve
description: A UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and one of East Africa's most beautiful marine parks, home to whale sharks, dolphins, sea turtles, and vibrant coral gardens near the historic town of Watamu.
